evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential
Entity of type FAQPage has no @id — cross-page interconnection blocked
FAQPage schema has 7 Questions but page shows ~14 FAQ pairs
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
Human voice vs committee 4/10 — Mostly committee-speak with scattered flashes of voice ('writing sentences an LLM can lift without embarrassment').
Genuine opinions vs safe claims 6/10 — A few sharp takes (E-E-A-T is theater, most AEO advice is garbage) exist but are surrounded by hedged, generic claims.
Unexpected phrasings 5/10 — A few vivid lines ('quotable source, period') are surrounded by predictable SEO-blog phrasing.
Point of view vs both-sides survey 5/10 — Has a POV in flashes but defaults to neutral explainer-comparison mode.
Quotable to a friend 6/10 — "The winners in AEO aren't the loudest—they're the most quotable" and the E-E-A-T line are genuinely quotable; rest isn't.
Intro earns attention in 2 sentences 3/10 — Opens with navigation crumbs, repeated titles, dateline, and a textbook definition—nothing earns attention.

intro_hook 6/10 — The post opens with a competent TL;DR but then immediately restates the definition verbatim in the first body sentence. There is no narrative tension, surprising statistic, or specific scenario to earn continued reading. The TL;DR itself is strong, but the actual opening sentence of the article body is a flat restatement that throat-clears rather than hooks.
